paper_says_column: 2507.23279 Eq. 6 + Algorithm 1: two-stage process — first detect MA-formation layers L, then global P_{99.5} AND 0.1·a_max AND l∈L — all three required
implementation_does_column: Per-layer z-score (mean + 2.5σ); no global statistics; no MA-formation layer pre-filtering; all 40 MoE layers profiled
justification_column: Avoids two-pass global stat collection and MA-pattern detection; per-layer z-score is conservative in practice (SEs produce z > 10 typically)

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 1 ... Stage 1: Calibration of MA-formation Layers ... if MA pattern detected ... L ← L ∪ {l} ... P99.5 ← Percentile99.5(A); amax ← max(A); if al,e > P99.5 and al,e > 1/10 amax then S ← S ∪ {(l,e)}

notes: Spec line 142 matches Implementation column. Paper Algorithm 1 confirms three-condition gate as quoted.

paper_says_column: Paper: purely threshold-based, no caps
implementation_does_column: max_blacklisted_per_layer=4 and global_blacklist_cap_pct=5%

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 1 lines 26-30 — only the threshold conditions (P99.5 + 0.1·a_max) gate inclusion in S; no per-layer or global cap appears.

notes: Caps verified at spec line 142. Paper Algorithm 1 has no analogous cap clause.

paper_says_column: 2604.06542 Eq. 10: γ∈[0,1], no default given
implementation_does_column: γ=0.1 (project-chosen, not from paper)
justification_column: Paper leaves γ unspecified; 0.1 chosen empirically

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Eb = E(1 − γ), (10) where γ ∈ [0, 1] is an entropy tolerance parameter.

notes: Paper §3 confirms γ∈[0,1] with no default. Spec line 160 sets γ=0.1.

paper_says_column: 2604.06542 Algorithm 1 lines 11–12: zero only pair entry D_{i*,j*} and D_{j*,i*}; update R^l ← R^l − 2·D_{i*,j*}
implementation_does_column: Zeros the absorbed expert's entire row and column in D^l; recomputes R^l from updated matrix
justification_column: Prevents the absorbed expert from influencing future pair-selection; the paper's update assumes the merged expert cannot be re-selected, but only zeroing the pair entry leaves stale similarity values that can distort R^l and layer selection in subsequent iterations

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 1 (lines 7-12) — pair (i*,j*) ← argmax D^l_{ij}; merge into C^l; update D^l and R^l accordingly. Paper text describes pairwise-entry zeroing rather than full row/col.

notes: Paper Algorithm 1 confirmed (sed lines 470-510). Spec deviation rationale is paper-internal and self-consistent.

paper_says_column: GRAPE has no floor constraints
implementation_does_column: min_experts_per_layer = num_routed_experts // 2 (=128); no early/late layer bonuses
justification_column: 50% max removal per layer bounds the compression within the range where papers demonstrate results; bonuses removed — the floor alone is sufficient

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 1 has no per-layer minimum-cluster floor; only global K target.

notes: Spec lines 107 and 170 set the floor; rationale is paper-internal.

paper_says_column: 2604.04356 §A.1: C=16 for Qwen3-30B at 25% reduction
implementation_does_column: max_merge_group_size=8 (with budget-bump fallback if exceeded)
justification_column: Conservative bound — caps any single centroid's absorption at half the paper's cap, keeping merge groups smaller and more homogeneous; the budget bump compensates for any blocked merges so the global expert target is still met

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Paper REAM §A.1 reports the merge-group cap C used for Qwen3-30B at 25% reduction (claims.jsonl entry hp_calib_size and §A appendix referenced).

notes: Spec line 279 matches Implementation column verbatim. Paper-internal rationale, no h200/resume tie-in.

paper_says_column: 2604.04356 Eq. 6: frequency-weighted average W_merged = Σ_i (freq_i / Σ_j freq_j) · W_i (no permutation)
implementation_does_column: Hungarian permutation P_i aligning each child expert's intermediate neurons to the centroid before averaging, with combined cost C = C_wt + C_act (gate+up Frobenius weight distance + per-neuron mean-activation L2 distance)
justification_column: Naive Eq. 6 averaging implicitly assumes intermediate neurons are index-aligned across experts. Qwen3 experts are independently initialised — by permutation symmetry of MLP hidden units (Git Re-Basin, Ainsworth et al. 2022), their intermediate-dim coordinate systems are arbitrary up to a permutation that the loss does not fix. Averaging without alignment therefore mixes uncorrelated neurons and inflates post-merge reconstruction error. Hungarian alignment recovers the correspondence before averaging. *TODO: A/B (with vs. without permutation) ablation pending Stage 6 evals.*

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: W_merged = (Σ_{i=1}^C S^freq_i W_i) / (Σ S^freq_i), (6), where W_i are expert i's weight matrices with neuron permutation alignment (Ainsworth et al., 2023) applied w.r.t. the dominant (centroid) expert.

notes: Ch.12 'Paper Says' parenthetical 'no permutation' is INCORRECT. Paper Eq. 6 explicitly states 'W_i ... with neuron permutation alignment (Ainsworth et al., 2023) applied w.r.t. the dominant (centroid) expert.' (source.md line 224-228). The spec's Hungarian alignment is therefore NOT a deviation from the paper at all — the paper itself prescribes neuron permutation alignment via the same Ainsworth/Git Re-Basin reference. The whole D5b row is misframed: paper and implementation agree on the principle. The deviation, if any, is the specific cost matrix C = C_wt + C_act (paper does not specify cost form). TODO ablation language → empirical_pending.

paper_says_column: 2604.02119 Theorem 3.2 requires cross-covariance for all linear layers
implementation_does_column: Cross-covariance C collected for gate_proj/up_proj (input-side) via dual-forward; down_proj falls back to Corollary 3.3 (B-only) because the teacher's per-expert intermediate activations require full expert dispatch instrumentation
justification_column: Gate/up inputs share the same hidden state (pre-routing) so one capture covers both; down_proj inputs are expert-internal (post gate+up) and differ between teacher and student expert sets

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Theorem 3.2 ... Corollary 3.3 (No distribution shift). If B = A, then AB⊤ = BB⊤ = LBL⊤ ... (source.md lines 625-715)

notes: Paper-internal rationale. NOTE: deeper paper-crossref findings (paper_2604.02119 deviation_uncovered: missing block-refinement, wrong layer objective vs paper recommendation) indicate D6 does NOT cover all 2604.02119 deviations — but those are MISSING ROWS, not flaws in D6 itself.

paper_says_column: 2509.25622 Eq. 7: ω = d₁ + n·d₂ (layers per group × dimensions)
implementation_does_column: ω = n_experts × (d_out + d_in)
justification_column: D-Rank targets shared-basis layer groups; adapted for MoE expert groups

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: ω = d1 + nd2, where n is the number of layers in the group ... kg ω = Tbudget

notes: Paper Eq. 7 form verified at source.md line 490. Spec adaptation to expert groups is internally consistent.

paper_says_column: 2509.25622: D-Rank Eq. 7 produces a single k_g per (layer, matrix_type) group; no per-projection-type multiplier
implementation_does_column: Group ranks from Eq. 7 are scaled by gate=1.33, up=0.67, down=1.0 (sum=3.0, budget-neutral on type-average) before per-expert redistribution
justification_column: Adapted from jangq's GGUF bit-allocation insight (gate:up:down ≈ 4:2:3, see 397B-MLP-ASYMMETRY.md §3.1): SwiGLU forward couples gate errors multiplicatively via SiLU, while down errors propagate to the residual stream. The ratio translates the same physical asymmetry from bit space to rank space. *TODO: empirical re-tune from clean per-projection recon_rel_err once Stage 6 evals are available; current values inherited unchanged from a prior bf16-bug-tainted run and are theoretically- (not empirically-) grounded.*

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: ω = d1 + nd2 ... kg ω = Tbudget — no per-projection-type multiplier appears in the optimization.

notes: TODO ablation language → empirical_pending. Cross-paper interaction with Swift-SVD per_proj_uniform claim (paper_2604.01609) is informational; D7a is sanctioned against D-Rank but Swift-SVD also prescribes uniformity — see MISSING-ROW for that secondary deviation.

paper_says_column: 2604.01609 Alg. 2: β = end-to-end layer importance, min-max normalized to [1,2]
implementation_does_column: β = per-expert spectral energy share (σ_i² / Σ σ_j²)
justification_column: Paper's β is per-layer importance (requires 40 extra forward passes); adapted to per-expert within-group redistribution where the paper has no solution. ε* is now activation-weighted via Stage 2 A-covariance (no longer a deviation)

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 2 Dynamic Rank Allocation Strategy ... end-to-end layer importance β ... (source.md lines 709, 717-722)

notes: Paper Alg 2 prescribes per-layer end-to-end β; spec adapts to per-expert spectral share. NOTE: paper crossref (paper_2604.01609 #beta_norm) flags missing min-max [1,2] shift in spec — this is a SEPARATE deviation not covered by D8 (D8 only addresses β source, not β normalization). Tracked as MISSING-ROW.

paper_says_column: 2410.21271 Alg. 1: full Q ∈ ℝ^{k×k} used; QQ^T = I guarantees Theorem 1 exactness
implementation_does_column: Eigenvectors below noise floor discarded; n_keep < k retained before SVD
justification_column: Suppresses near-zero noise directions; weakens Theorem 1 exactness but improves numerical stability

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 1 Eigenspace low-rank approximation (EoRA): step 2 'Run Eigendecomposition on X̃X̃^T = QΛQ^T'; step 3 'Reformulate Λ = (√Λ)(√Λ); QQ^T = ...' — Q is full eigenbasis, no truncation specified.

notes: Spec lines 430, 463-465 confirm noise-floor truncation. Paper Alg 1 verified at source.md line 284-296.

paper_says_column: 2603.02217 §F.3 Table 1: calibration dataset = c4 (used identically across all experiments)
implementation_does_column: Multi-domain Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data with weighted subsets (chat 0.56, math 0.21, science 0.11, etc.)
justification_column: Task-aware calibration better matches target deployment distribution; c4 is general pre-training data with limited reasoning/code coverage

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: F.3. Router Knowledge Distillation Hyperparameter Settings ... Note. The Router KD hyperparameters and the calibration dataset (c4) (Dodge et al., 2021) are identical across all experimental cases. (See Table 1) ... Calibration dataset c4

notes: Paper §F.3 Table 1 verified verbatim. Spec line 68 sets Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data.

paper_says_column: 2604.02119 §3.3 / Algorithm 2 / §6 ablation: AA-SVD's most critical contribution is BLOCK-LEVEL refinement of (W_i, θ_i) optimizing block-output loss; per-layer factorization without it underperforms the paper's headline numbers (Table 1 vs Table 5).
implementation_does_column: Stage 3 implements only per-layer anchored-adaptive factorization (Path 1, objective (4) ‖WX − W'X'‖²_F via Theorem 3.2); no block-refinement step, no θ_i optimization. §10 'Protected Components' explicitly forbids modifying RMSNorm scales — directly contradicting paper's prescription that θ_i be tuned during refinement.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Theorem 3.2 + Algorithm 2 (block-iterative refinement) + §6 ablation showing block refinement dominates layer objective. See paper crossref claims fig2_block_loss, alg2_refine, ablation_refine_dominant, paper_recommendation, sec3_3_theta.

notes: Paper crossref flags 8 deviation_uncovered claims for 2604.02119 around block refinement and the paper's actual recommendation (input-aware + refinement). D6 only sanctions cross-covariance scope; the larger architectural omission (no Path 2 / no block refinement / contradiction with §10 Protected Components) is uncovered by §12. Severity: critical (paper authors rank block-refinement above layer objective).

paper_says_column: 2604.01609 §3.2 / Alg. 2: minimal-rank floor k_i ← k̄·δ with δ=0.5 (paper warns δ=0 is unstable); flexible-pool b = k̄·L − Σk_i for redistribution.
implementation_does_column: Spec Phase B redistributes k_g·N_experts proportionally to s_i (β_i^α) with no minimal-rank floor and no δ parameter — equivalent to running the paper's δ=0 unstable regime.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 2 Dynamic Rank Allocation Strategy includes minimal-rank floor k̄·δ; paper warns δ=0 is unstable, recommends δ=0.5. (paper crossref minimal_rank, delta_role, alg2_minimal_rank_def, flex_pool — all deviation_uncovered, severity high)

notes: Severity: high. Spec runs the paper's explicitly-warned unstable regime (δ=0). No §12 row sanctions this. Should add D-row or implement δ floor.

paper_says_column: 2604.01609 Alg. 2: β min-max normalized to [1,2] before exponentiation (β_i^α).
implementation_does_column: Spec Phase B uses raw β_i = σ_i²/Σσ_j² ∈ [0,1] without the +1 / min-max shift to [1,2]; β_i^α can collapse to ~0 for small spectral shares, distorting score dynamic range.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Paper §3.2 normalizes β to [1,2] before α exponentiation (paper crossref beta_norm — deviation_uncovered, severity medium).

notes: D8 sanctions β SOURCE (per-expert spectral share) but is silent on β NORMALIZATION. Severity: medium.

paper_says_column: 2509.25622 D-Rank: effective rank computed from singular values of the WHITENED matrix S·W (Eq. 1 uses λ^i_g = σ_i(S_g·W_g)²), where S_g = cholesky(X^T X). Paper mandates FP64 for S.
implementation_does_column: Spec Phase B computes p_i = σ_i²/Σσ_j² and R_eff directly from raw weight singular values; no S whitening matrix is computed. Eigendecomposition replaces Cholesky in §6 (line 429), but for AA-SVD's B-cov, not D-Rank's S. Spec uses bf16/fp16/fp32 — no FP64 path, despite H200 supporting FP64 Tensor Core.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: D-Rank Eq. 1: λ^i_g = σ_i(S_g·W_g)²; S_g = cholesky(X^T X); FP64 mandated. (paper crossref prelim_whitening, post_eq7_pipeline, hp_hardware, whitening_S_definition — all deviation_uncovered, severity high)

notes: D7 sanctions ω form only; the underlying R_eff input (whitened S·W vs raw W) is a SEPARATE high-severity deviation that no §12 row covers. FP64 omission also lacks h200 justification (h200_truth.md confirms H200 supports FP64 Tensor Core — no hardware reason to drop it). Mixed paper+h200 regime.

paper_says_column: 2410.21271 EoRA: input is averaged FIRST, then outer product forms X̃X̃^T (rank-1 from mean vector); Algorithm 1 step 2 inputs 'X̃: Average of the input activations of the current layer over the calibration set'.
implementation_does_column: Spec uses input AUTO-covariance A = X^T X accumulated across calibration tokens (full second-moment, rank up to d_in), reused from Stage 2; notation 'A = X̃X̃^T' is misleading.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 1 Input: X̃: Average of the input activations of the current layer over the calibration set. (paper crossref claim_xtilde_mean — deviation_uncovered, severity high)

notes: Material numerical deviation: rank-1 mean-outer-product vs full second-moment covariance changes the eigenspace fundamentally. No §12 row. Severity: high.

paper_says_column: 2410.21271: paper default compensation rank ~128 per layer, across all matrices (no per-expert per-matrix cap of 64).
implementation_does_column: Spec caps EoRA rank at compensation_budget_pct=3% of Stage 3 savings, capped at eigenspace_rank_cap=64 per expert per matrix.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Paper default rank ~128 (paper crossref hp_default_rank — deviation_uncovered, severity medium).

notes: Likely VRAM/budget-driven (256-expert × 64-rank correction is already large) but no h200 fact backs it explicitly. Severity: medium. Spec should sanction with D-row citing h200 VRAM bound.

paper_says_column: 2604.04356 REAM: 3072 calibration sequences × 512 tokens.
implementation_does_column: Spec Stage 2 uses 1024 calibration sequences (per spec line 274).

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Paper REAM uses 3072 sequences × 512 tokens (paper crossref hp_calib_size, claim_calib_seqs_3072_x_512 — deviation_uncovered, severity medium).

notes: Calibration size discrepancy not covered by D11 (D11 is for paper 2603.02217 c4 vs Nemotron). Severity: medium.

paper_says_column: 2604.04356 REAM: evaluated 'without any fine-tuning after compression' (one-shot static).
implementation_does_column: Spec Stage 2.5 trains mlp.gate.weight (router KD) after merging — post-compression training, departs from REAM's static protocol.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: REAM evaluated without fine-tuning (paper crossref claim_no_finetune, claim_static_only — deviation_uncovered).

notes: Cross-paper deviation: spec blends REAM (static) with 2603.02217 (Router KD); §12 should explicitly note the protocol mixing. Severity: low.

paper_says_column: 2604.01609 Swift-SVD+ Algorithm 2 indexes only over layers i ∈ {1..L}, prescribing per-projection uniformity (no per-projection-type rank multiplier).
implementation_does_column: Spec applies per-projection multipliers gate=1.33, up=0.67, down=1.0 — same as D7a, but D7a's 'Paper Says' field cites 2509.25622 (D-Rank), not 2604.01609 (Swift-SVD).

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Algorithm 2 indexes layers i ∈ {1..L} only, no projection-type loop (paper crossref per_proj_uniform — deviation_uncovered, severity medium, sanction_justification=ch12)

notes: D7a sanctions per-projection bias against D-Rank only; Swift-SVD also prescribes uniformity. Severity: medium. Either extend D7a's 'Paper Says' to cite both papers or add a cross-reference.

paper_says_column: Stage 2 covariance numerics: paper Swift-SVD certifies exact-optimum stability in FP32 only.
implementation_does_column: Spec stores Stage 2 covariance in FP16 (line 274 explicit) for VRAM/storage on H200.

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: Paper Swift-SVD certifies FP32 stability; spec uses FP16 (paper crossref fp_precision_table5 — deviation_uncovered, severity low, sanction_justification=h200)

notes: h200 justification (FP16 to save VRAM) is plausible — h200_truth.md confirms BF16/FP16 throughput parity (1979 TFLOPS) — but no §12 row sanctions this with explicit h200 citation. Severity: low.

paper_says_column: 2510.13999 REAP: calibration source = 50/50 mixture of allenai/c4 + theblackcat102/evol-codealpaca-v1.
implementation_does_column: Spec Stage 2 uses 1024 sequences from nvidia/Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data (multi-domain weighted).

paper_says_evidence.verbatim: REAP calibration is c4 + evol-codealpaca (paper crossref hp-calib-small — deviation_uncovered, severity low).

notes: D11 covers Router KD (2603.02217); REAP calibration source is a separate change. Severity: low. Could fold into D11 by extending paper citation list.
